title: Post-quantum cryptography without quanta
summary: Explainer from Poland's National Institute of Telecommunications (Instytut Łączności, a state research institute) introducing post-quantum cryptography and clarifying that it relies on classical methods resistant to quantum attacks rather than on quantum mechanics.

title: Post-quantum cryptography - seminar with Prof. Kris Gaj
summary: Announcement by Poland's National Institute of Telecommunications of a post-quantum cryptography seminar held on 27 June 2025 featuring Prof. Kris Gaj.

title: Poland at the forefront of quantum technology solutions - QKD presentation
summary: NASK (Poland's national research institute and CSIRT operator) news item presenting quantum technology work, naming post-quantum cryptography (PQC) and quantum key distribution (QKD) as responses to quantum threats.

title: Efficient post-quantum cryptography from the perspective of resource consumption
summary: Polish government research-information system (RAD-on) project profile describing work on PQC migration paths across different deployment contexts. Flagged for review: this is a research-project database record rather than a formal institutional document.

title: USA - National Cybersecurity Strategy (NASK Cyberpolicy analysis)
summary: NASK Cyberpolicy commentary on the US National Cybersecurity Strategy, noting post-quantum cryptography among priority technologies. Flagged for review: issuer is domestic (NASK) but the document analyses another country's strategy rather than a Polish PQC document.

title: Polska w erze kwantowej, czyli założenia polityki rozwoju technologii kwantowych
summary: Poland's Ministry of Digital Affairs published the foundational assumptions for a national quantum-technology development policy on 29 August 2025 (public consultation to 30 September 2025), structured across five pillars and three phases to 2035 and explicitly naming post-quantum security (bezpieczeństwo postkwantowe) and post-quantum cryptography among its priorities. First comprehensive national-level policy framing of Poland's quantum-safe posture.

title: Perspektywy na lata 2026-2030 (Instytut Łączności)
issuingOrg: Instytut Łączności – PIB (National Institute of Telecommunications)
summary: Strategic research-direction outlook for 2026-2030 from Poland's National Institute of Telecommunications (IŁ-PIB), which explicitly plans 'ocena bezpieczeństwa rozwiązań z zakresu kryptografii postkwantowej' (security assessment of post-quantum cryptography solutions) alongside e-identity/EUDI Wallet conformance work. Confirms a forward research commitment to PQC evaluation at a state research institute.

title: Seminar with Prof. Kris Gaj: Post-Quantum Digital Signature Standards and Hardware Implementations
issuingOrg: Instytut Łączności – PIB (National Institute of Telecommunications)
summary: English-language announcement by Poland's National Institute of Telecommunications (IŁ-PIB) of a 27 June 2025 seminar with Prof. Kris Gaj on post-quantum cryptographic digital-signature schemes (ML-DSA, SLH-DSA) and their hardware implementations. This is the English counterpart of the Polish seminar page already in the corpus, at a distinct URL.

title: Quantum technologies and data protection – conference
issuingOrg: UODO (Urząd Ochrony Danych Osobowych / Personal Data Protection Office)
summary: Report by Poland's Personal Data Protection Office (UODO) on its conference on quantum technologies and data protection, explicitly discussing the use of post-quantum cryptography in digital-identity wallets and blockchain and the need to develop post-quantum software against the quantum threat. Flagged for human review: issuer is a domestic regulator but uodo.gov.pl is not on the trusted-domains list, the publication date could not be verified, and the item is a conference report rather than a substantive PQC policy or guidance document.
